name: Stir Fried Pork Mixture for Chow or Lo Mein
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak dried mushrooms.
2. Slice the pork into thin strips or cut it into small pieces. Slice the celery, onion, and soaked mushrooms. Cut the scallion stalks into 1-inch pieces.
3. Heat oil in a pan. Add salt, then add scallions and stir-fry until translucent.
4. Add the pork; stir-fry until it loses its pink color (2 to 3 minutes). Sprinkle with soy sauce and sugar and mix well.
5. Add celery, onion, and mushrooms; stir-fry for an additional 2 minutes.
6. Add stock and heat quickly. Cook uncovered for another 2 to 3 minutes over medium heat. Combine with soft-fried or tossed noodles, as indicated below.
Variations:
Shred the pork and vegetables. Omit the stock and additional cooking in step 5. Chinese noodles are distinguished not only by their ingredients but also by how they're prepared. The noodles can be cooked, mixed, blended, and combined with various meats, seafood, and vegetables. They can be added to soup or served alone with toppings.
Note: Lo Mein means "tossed or mixed noodles" and requires parboiled noodles (previously drained and chilled) to be added directly to the meat and vegetable combinations, which have already been stir-fried. These noodles are tossed or mixed and heated through with the cooked combinations, resulting in a moister dish with more sauce than Chow Mein.
